title: Explain Scripts
short_answer: Scripts are helper files used to start, test, clean, or diagnose the app.
text: In this app, scripts should make repeated actions easy and predictable. Use scripts instead of manually guessing long commands.

title: Explain Start Script
short_answer: Start Script launches part of the app.
text: A start script can launch UI, assistant loop, STT loop, or a full play mode. It should make startup repeatable.

title: Explain Test Script
short_answer: Test Script checks whether a block works.
text: A test script should verify one block or flow without needing the entire app. Use test scripts after changing HELP, memory, LLM, STT, or speech output.

title: Explain Smoke Test
short_answer: Smoke Test is a quick proof that a block is alive.
text: Smoke tests do not prove every detail. They prove that imports, basic flow, and one expected result are working.

title: Explain Clean Script
short_answer: Clean Script removes temporary or generated files safely.
text: Clean scripts are useful when runtime or build files become noisy. They should never delete important source files or user data.

title: Explain Maintenance Script
short_answer: Maintenance Script keeps the project organized or healthy.
text: Maintenance scripts can clean runtime, check files, validate JSON, archive logs, or prepare folders. They are not main app features, but they protect the workflow.

title: Explain PowerShell
short_answer: PowerShell is the Windows terminal used to run project commands.
text: This app is Windows-first for many commands. Use PowerShell to activate the venv, start the app, run scripts, and inspect files.

title: Explain Venv
short_answer: Venv is the Python virtual environment for the app.
text: The venv keeps Python packages isolated for this project. Before running Python scripts, activate the correct venv from the project root.

title: Explain Start
short_answer: Start means launching the app or one of its loops.
text: Start commands should be simple and repeatable. The goal is to launch the UI, assistant loop, STT loop, or full play setup without manual confusion.

title: Explain Diagnostics
short_answer: Diagnostics means checking whether the app is healthy.
text: Diagnostics can inspect runtime state, logs, service status, LLM availability, memory readiness, audio devices, or queue movement.
